name: New Caledonia
conservationEfforts: Lagoons of New Caledonia are a UNESCO World Heritage Site. Province-level environmental protection agencies manage conservation. Kagu recovery program has stabilized populations. Invasive species control programs target deer, pigs, and rats.
description:
New Caledonia, a French territory in the southwest Pacific, is a continental fragment that separated from Australia 80 million years ago. This ancient isolation has produced one of the world's most distinctive floras, with 74% of plant species found nowhere else.

The island is home to Amborella trichopoda, considered the most ancient living flowering plant lineage on Earth. The kagu, a nearly flightless bird found only on New Caledonia, and the New Caledonian crow, famous for its remarkable tool-making abilities, are conservation icons. The island's ultramafic soils support unique plant communities adapted to high nickel and chromium concentrations.
